qa_pairs.question: Why is it important to perform scaling tests before running Ansys Mechanical production jobs?
qa_pairs.answer: Scaling tests should be performed to ensure that the optimal number of cores and minimum amount memory are specified in your scripts.

qa_pairs.question: Which type of Ansys Mechanical script is generally recommended for better performance?
qa_pairs.answer: The single-node (SMP Shared Memory Parallel) scripts will typically perform better than the multinode (DIS Distributed Memory Parallel) scripts and should therefore be used whenever possible.

qa_pairs.question: When should single-node (SMP) scripts be used for Ansys Mechanical jobs?
qa_pairs.answer: Single-node (SMP Shared Memory Parallel) scripts should be used whenever possible because they typically perform better than multinode (DIS Distributed Memory Parallel) scripts.

qa_pairs.question: How can I avoid compatibility issues with Ansys Mechanical jobs?
qa_pairs.answer: To help avoid compatibility issues, the Ansys module loaded in your script should ideally match the version used to generate the input file.
